findings.message: Hardcoded secret committed in source: `api_secret_key` defaults to the literal string "super-secret-key-do-not-share". Any value checked into version control must be treated as compromised; if this key is used to sign tokens, authenticate API calls, or encrypt data, an attacker with repo access (or anyone who later sees the git history) can forge/decrypt that material. It also bypasses the file's own stated invariant ("All configuration, sourced from environment / .env. Never hardcode config.").
findings.suggestion: Remove the default value and require it from the environment, e.g. `api_secret_key: SecretStr = Field(...)` (no default, so pydantic-settings will fail fast if APP_API_SECRET_KEY is unset) and use `SecretStr` so it does not leak via `repr()`/logs. Rotate the exposed value and purge it from git history (e.g. `git filter-repo`).
